description_long: This is a large, irregularly shaped rock with a rugged surface and prominent angular facets. The upper portions are covered with patches of green moss or lichen, giving it a natural, weathered appearance. The rock's color is predominantly gray, with the green areas concentrated on flatter and more exposed surfaces. Deep grooves and striations are visible on some faces, suggesting erosion or glacial activity. The overall form is asymmetrical, with one side appearing steeper and more vertical, while the other sides slope more gently. Its size and texture make it a substantial obstacle or natural feature suitable for urban landscaping or as a barrier.
description: A large, gray rock with green moss patches and a rugged, angular surface, featuring deep grooves and an asymmetrical form.
description_view_0: This view highlights the top and one sloped side, showing extensive green moss coverage and a rough, uneven surface.
description_view_1: This side reveals a steep, mostly bare gray face with vertical grooves and minimal moss.
description_view_2: This angle shows a broad, gently sloping surface with a dense patch of green moss and some exposed rock.
description_view_3: This perspective emphasizes the mossy, rounded top and a shadowed, rugged base with less visible detail.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped public space
